items.headline: City Council to review zoning and protections for parks and recreational sites
items.summary: The City Council is holding a study session to examine existing designations, zoning corrections, and state law protections for recreational facilities and parkland sites. This session will help determine if current zoning and protections align with state requirements and city needs.

items.headline: Sunnyvale to study banning chemical pesticides on city property
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission is reviewing a policy to eliminate chemical pesticide use on all city-owned or leased property. This would affect parks, medians, municipal buildings, and other public spaces.

items.headline: City to study artificial turf policy
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission is studying the city's artificial turf policy. The review could affect decisions about which parks and recreation areas use synthetic grass versus natural fields.

items.headline: Echo Place development cleared to build 6 homes and 2 duplexes
items.summary: The Miller development on Echo Place receives permits for 4 single-family homes, 2 duplexes, and 1 additional single-family home, adding 9 residential units to the neighborhood. All permits issued the same day suggests a coordinated residential infill project.

items.headline: Restaurant at 407 Lytton gets permit for backyard dining expansion
items.summary: A Lytton Avenue restaurant is permitted to add outdoor dining for up to 20 patrons with artificial landscaping and additional seating in its backyard. This expands the restaurant's capacity without requiring a full renovation permit.

items.headline: Historic house to be retained; four townhomes planned at Crescent Avenue
items.summary: The Heritage Preservation Commission will consider a resource alteration permit for 434 Crescent Avenue on July 1, 2026. The proposal would add four three-story townhomes to the rear while keeping the existing 960-square-foot historic house on the site.

items.headline: Los Gatos to hold municipal election November 3, 2026
items.summary: The City Council adopted a resolution calling a general municipal election for November 3, 2026, to fill three seats. This is a routine election cycle for the city.

items.headline: Final map approved for Tract 10675 on Boulevard
items.summary: The Council approved the final subdivision map for Tract 10675 located on Boulevard. The agenda does not specify whether this enables new residential or commercial development or what the tract contains.
